subject: Sociology
title: Weber and the Protestant Ethic
description: One-minute skill: Weber and the Protestant Ethic.

hook.text: Capitalism thrived in Protestant nations first. Max Weber asked if that was just a coincidence.

buildup.text: Calvinists believed in predestination — God already chose who's saved. Anxiety about salvation was intense.

discovery.text: To cope, they worked hard and saved money, seeing worldly success as a sign of divine favor.

twist.text: This religious anxiety accidentally created the disciplined mindset that capitalism needed to flourish.

climax.text: Weber showed that ideas, not just economics, drive history. Religion shaped the modern economic system.

quiz.question: What did Weber argue about Protestantism and capitalism?
